original_problem: In a small town, there are three switches, but they are not labelled. Each switch corresponds to one of three light bulbs in a room. Each light bulb is either on or off. You can turn the switches on and off as many times as you want, but you can only enter the room one time to observe the light bulbs. How can you figure out which switch controls which light bulb?
deconstructed_problem: In a small town, there are three switches, but they are not labelled. Each switch corresponds to one of three light bulbs in a room. Each light bulb is either on or off. You can turn the switches on and off as many times as you want, but you can only enter the room one time to observe the light bulbs. How can you figure out which switch controls which light bulb?
original_steps: Step 1: Turn switch 1 to the 'on' position for 5 minutes to heat up the corresponding bulb.
Step 2: Turn switch 1 to the 'off' position and turn switch 2 to the 'on' position.
Step 3: Immediately enter the room to observe the bulbs. Identify the bulb that is on (switch 2), the bulb that is off but warm (switch 1), and the bulb that is off and cold (switch 3).
skeleton_steps: ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 1: Turn switch 1 to the 'on' position for 5 minutes to heat up the corresponding bulb.
ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 2: Turn switch 1 to the 'off' position and turn switch 2 to the 'on' position.
ପଦକ୍ଷେପ (step) 3: Immediately enter the room to observe the bulbs. Identify the bulb that is on (switch 2), the bulb that is off but warm (switch 1), and the bulb that is off and cold (switch 3).
final_answer: The switch that was turned to the 'on' position first (switch 1) corresponds to the warm but off bulb, the switch that was turned to the 'on' position last (switch 2) corresponds to the bulb that is on, and the remaining switch (switch 3) corresponds to the cold and off bulb.
